ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002708 covers ramen noodle soup as a category; no specific code exists for chicken-bone-broth ramen with special toppings. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FHR represents chicken-based noodle soup as the nearest defensible parent category. No specific torikoku ramen code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for ramen or torikoku ramen specifically. SNOMED CT does not cover prepared ethnic noodle dishes at this granularity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of Torikoku Special Ramen weighing approximately 550 g, comprising noodles, broth, chicken chashu, soft-boiled egg, bamboo shoots, nori, corn, and green onions.

nutrient_source.title: Ramen, chicken broth with noodles and toppings — composite estimate based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for chicken broth, wheat noodles (cooked), chicken (roasted), egg (hard-boiled), bamboo shoots, and seaweed
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Torikoku Special Ramen. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries: cooked wheat ramen noodles (FDC 168936), chicken broth (FDC 171534), roasted chicken breast (FDC 171477), hard-boiled egg (FDC 173424), canned bamboo shoots (FDC 169226), dried nori (FDC 168456), and corn kernels (FDC 169998). Proportions estimated for a 550 g bowl: noodles 130 g, broth 280 g, chicken 60 g, egg 50 g, bamboo shoots 15 g, nori 5 g, corn 10 g. Values scaled to per-100 g basis. Sodium elevated due to soy-sauce tare and seasoned broth.

gi_source.title: Noodles, instant, wheat-based, cooked — GI reference; and Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Torikoku Special Ramen. GI estimated at 55 (medium) based on the GI of wheat-based ramen noodles (GI ~55, University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international tables). The chicken broth and protein-rich toppings (egg, chicken) are known to attenuate postprandial glucose response, supporting a medium GI classification. Glycemic load calculated as: (GI 55 × carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (55 × 61.6 g) / 100 ≈ 34.0. Carbohydrate per serving = 11.2 g per 100 g × 550 g = 61.6 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from soy-sauce tare and seasoned chicken bone broth
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of Torikoku Special Ramen due to its estimated sodium content of approximately 420 mg per 100 g (approximately 2310 mg per bowl), which substantially exceeds the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; requesting reduced-sodium tare or consuming only a portion of the broth is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index wheat noodles with moderate carbohydrate load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that Torikoku Special Ramen carries a medium glycemic index of approximately 55 and a glycemic load of approximately 34 per bowl; portion control of the noodle component and pairing with the protein-rich toppings (chicken, egg) can help moderate postprandial blood glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate saturated fat from chicken skin and egg yolk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should moderate intake of Torikoku Special Ramen given its high sodium load and moderate saturated fat content (approximately 0.7 g per 100 g); choosing lean chicken toppings, limiting broth consumption, and reducing frequency of consumption aligns with heart-healthy dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Torikoku Special Ramen is a Japanese chicken bone broth ramen dish served at Ramen Matsuri in Singapore. 'Torikoku' (鶏骨) refers to chicken bones used to prepare a rich, milky-white or golden broth through prolonged simmering, yielding a deeply flavoured, collagen-rich soup base. The 'Special' designation indicates a premium topping set typically including chicken chashu (braised or roasted chicken slices), a soft-boiled seasoned egg (ajitsuke tamago), bamboo shoots (menma), dried nori, corn kernels, and sliced green onions, served over springy wheat ramen noodles. The broth is seasoned with a soy-sauce tare and finished with sesame oil, producing a savoury, umami-forward flavour profile with a silky mouthfeel. Nutritionally, the dish provides moderate protein from chicken and egg, moderate carbohydrates from wheat noodles, and is notably high in sodium due to the seasoned broth and tare. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (medium), making portion control of the noodle component relevant for blood glucose management. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with gluten or egg allergies. It is a popular lunch and dinner option in Japanese ramen restaurants across Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Torikoku Special Ramen?
Is Torikoku Ramen high in sodium?
What toppings come with Torikoku Special?
Is Torikoku Ramen suitable for diabetics?
Is chicken bone broth ramen healthy?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Torikoku Special Ramen. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for the component ingredients at estimated proportions. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (wheat ramen noodles) in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level USDA SR Legacy data is high quality and the proportions are reasonable for a standard ramen bowl.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reliably reported across the USDA SR Legacy component entries used for this composite estimate and is not meaningfully contributed by the primary ingredients at these proportions. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not consistently reported in the USDA SR Legacy entries for chicken broth and cooked wheat noodles used in this composite. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as selenium content varies substantially by geographic origin of wheat and chicken and is not reported in the specific USDA SR Legacy entries used. Trans fat is 0.0 g per 100 g; this is a confirmed trace value consistent with the absence of partially hydrogenated oils in the ingredient set. Sodium is elevated (approximately 420 mg per 100 g; approximately 2310 mg per bowl) due to soy-sauce tare and seasoned broth — this is a primary clinical concern. GI set to 55 (medium) as an analogous estimate based on wheat ramen noodles per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish exists. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type as no exact codes exist for torikoku ramen in FoodOn or FoodEx2. Price from field survey data source record: SGD 22.13 (single restaurant: Ramen Matsuri, 7 North Canal Road). This record is a GD-series food (field survey-sourced) and is not present in the FD-series food index.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is 'Torikoku Special Ramen', a precise canonical English name. Japanese local name '鶏骨スペシャルラーメン' is present, satisfying G6 for Japanese cuisine.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: All four core macros present and numeric: energy_kcal 89.0, protein_g 5.8, fat_g 2.4, carbohydrate_g 11.2. Source is USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite estimate, clearly documented.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g 0.6, sodium_mg 420.0, saturated_fat_g 0.7, sugar_g 0.8 — all present as numeric values and plausible for a chicken broth ramen dish.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index 55 (numeric, >0), gi_category 'medium', glycemic_load_per_serving 34.0, gi_source documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed journal for wheat ramen noodles as closest analogous food. Estimation basis fully documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_00002708 (ramen noodle soup) used as closest_parent with gap note. FoodEx2 A0FHR (noodle soup, chicken-based) used as closest_parent with gap note. SNOMED CT is null with gap note explaining absence of suitable code. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters, explains what the food is, preparation method, taste and texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients. Common user queries and meal context populated.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (high concern, sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (moderate concern, GI and carbohydrate load), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate concern, sodium and saturated fat). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' used correctly.
